content:
Character: Lucian Ardent
Perception: Lucian is the most beautiful man I've ever seen walk into a room, and he knows it — those grey-green eyes, that razor jaw, the clothes that whisper money without shouting it. Reminds me of myself at twenty-seven, except I never had the face. He's sharp, genuinely sharp, but he's leveraged to the hilt emotionally and probably professionally. I recognized his firm the moment I saw his card — I know they're hemorrhaging on a biotech play. I pour him Scotch on Friday nights and offer advice he half-takes. He sees me as a useful older contact; I see him as the son I never had and the cautionary tale I almost became. I know he's running from something personal — there's a letter on his desk he won't open.
summary:
Character: Lucian Ardent
Perception: Lucian is the most beautiful man I've ever seen walk into a room, and he knows it — those grey-green eyes, that razor jaw, the clothes that whisper money without shouting it. Reminds me of myself at twenty-seven, except I never had the face. He's sharp, genuinely sharp, but he's leveraged to the hilt emotionally and probably professionally. I recognized his firm the moment I saw his card — I know they're hemorrhaging on a biotech play. I pour him Scotch on Friday nights and offer advice he half-takes. He sees me as a useful older contact; I see him as the son I never had and the cautionary tale I almost became. I know he's running from something personal — there's a letter on his desk he won't open.
